metaLayer.summary: Internal rules, intent table, vague-word clarification matrix, confusion zones, soft-CTA library, contacts directory, staff routing targets (shadow), hours+pricing with 4-tier confidence, canonical link directory.

plannedSections: sports (basketball, club-de-course, triathlon, pickleball, squash, salles-entrainement)
clinique-sportive (therapie-sportive, massotherapie, osteopathie, physiotherapie, nutrition, services-medicaux)

tablesViaVision.summary: 48 pages flagged by Phase A heuristic as needing vision-based extraction (schedule grids + price tables). Phase B script will render only these to PNG and re-extract structurally.

leadRouting.note: All staff emails in staff.json are routed to shadowRecipients until Daphné signs off. Flip to real recipients in one place by changing mode to 'live'.

_consumptionContract: The prompt builder (apps/api/src/prompts/maa-chat-system-v2.ts) reads from this folder. answerMaaChat() opts into v2 via env flag KNOWLEDGE_VERSION=v2. With the flag off, v1 stays live.

bilingualPolicy.source: Daphné's PDF is 95% French (only ~12 of 203 pages contain English snippets like 'see schedule on PDF'). She has not yet sent an English version of the structured rules.
bilingualPolicy.behaviour: The LLM translates Daphné's French clarification questions / soft CTAs / confusion-zone rules to natural Quebec-English on the fly. Explicit translation anchors are baked into the v2 prompt (bilingualBlock) so the LLM doesn't drift.
bilingualPolicy.urlPolicy: When the visitor is reading English and a link has a /fr/ path, the bot may swap to the /en/ counterpart ONLY when confident the page exists. PDFs are typically bilingual or French; never invent slug translations.
bilingualPolicy.futureWork: If Daphné produces an English-language version of the rules+intents+clarifications later, we add labelEn/clarificationQuestionEn/etc. fields to the JSON files and the loader exposes them per locale.

notes: Populated section-by-section as Claude reads Daphné's PDF. Meta layer is the highest-leverage payload — it alone makes the bot substantially smarter than v1. Operational sheets are encoded over subsequent passes.
